# UC-14.9.8 · Controller Firmware Activation Monitoring

## Description

Firmware activation on a controller changes the software running the physical process. Unauthorized firmware changes are a key indicator of advanced OT attacks (e.g., Stuxnet-style). OT security platforms detect firmware activation events across supported industrial protocols and generate Critical-severity alerts.

## Value

Firmware activation on a controller changes the software running the physical process. Unauthorized firmware changes are a key indicator of advanced OT attacks (e.g., Stuxnet-style). OT security platforms detect firmware activation events across supported industrial protocols and generate Critical-severity alerts.

## Implementation

Both platforms detect firmware activation events on controllers. Alert immediately — this is always high-priority. Cross-reference with approved change records. Investigate source and target. Validate firmware version matches approved versions.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) · `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=cisco:cybervision:syslog` · Nozomi: `sourcetype=nozomi:alert` (type_id="PROTOCOL-ENGINEERING").
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Both platforms detect firmware activation events on controllers. Alert immediately — this is always high-priority. Cross-reference with approved change records. Investigate source and target. Validate firmware version matches approved versions.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=nozomi sourcetype="nozomi:alert" type_id="PROTOCOL-ENGINEERING-FIRMWARE*"
| table _time, name, src_ip, dst_ip, zone, risk
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Controller Firmware Activation Monitoring** — Firmware activation on a controller changes the software running the physical process. Unauthorized firmware changes are a key indicator of advanced OT attacks (e.g., Stuxnet-style). OT security platforms detect firmware activation events across supported industrial protocols and generate Critical-severity alerts.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=cisco:cybervision:syslog` · Nozomi: `sourcetype=nozomi:alert` (type_id="PROTOCOL-ENGINEERING"). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) · `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: nozomi; **sourcetype**: nozomi:alert. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=nozomi, sourcetype="nozomi:alert". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Pipeline stage (see **Controller Firmware Activation Monitoring**): table _time, name, src_ip, dst_ip, zone, risk
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Run the search for a time range that should include both routine and change activity, then compare the same assets, flows, and alerts in the Cisco Cyber Vision UI and in Nozomi Guardian or CMC, matching whichever source the event came from. Reconcile severities, counts, and key fields in both tools.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Firmware activation event log; target controller details; change authorization correlation.

## SPL

```spl
index=nozomi sourcetype="nozomi:alert" type_id="PROTOCOL-ENGINEERING-FIRMWARE*"
| table _time, name, src_ip, dst_ip, zone, risk
| sort -_time
```

## Visualization

Firmware activation event log; target controller details; change authorization correlation.
